metadata.description: Banking-grade transaction intelligence configuration with entity normalization, fuzzy matching, and confidence scoring

rules: Entity interpretation overrides all other classifications
Direction rule overrides generic keyword when entity is detected
Entity alias match has priority over standalone keywords
Exact keyword match preferred over fuzzy match
Longer keyword match preferred over shorter
Higher confidence score wins within same group
Group priority order breaks ties across groups
High confidence keywords override low confidence
Pattern match used as supporting evidence, not primary classifier

behavior_context.description: Behavioral pattern detection for ML integration - configuration only, no implementation

feedback_learning.description: User feedback and learning system - configuration only, no implementation

entity_interpretation._description: Entity-based classification that runs BEFORE keyword matching
entity_interpretation._usage: Match entity name in transaction narration, then apply credit/debit interpretation

BUSINESS_ENTITIES._description: Business entity tokens for enterprise detection

OTA_MERCHANTS._description: Online Travel Agencies - credit means OTA income (host/partner payout)

BANKS._description: Bank entities - just TRANSFER, direction doesn't matter for classification

PAYMENT_PLATFORMS._description: Payment platforms and UPI apps - simple UPI_TRANSFER, direction doesn't apply

UPI_HANDLES._description: UPI handle patterns - simple UPI_TRANSFER, direction doesn't apply

PAYMENT_GATEWAYS._description: Payment gateway entities for merchant settlements

TERMINAL_SETTLEMENTS._description: Generic terminal card settlement events. Direction-aware: credit = business income (merchant payout/settlement), debit = business expense (card settlement from customer).

classification._description: Keyword-based classification from original words.json - use for non-entity matching

BUSINESS_VENDORS._description: Generic business vendor detection based on company suffixes. Direction-aware: credit=business income, debit=business expense.

SALARY_PAYMENTS._description: Salary payments. Direction-aware: credit=salary income, debit=salary expense (business paying salary).

ATW_TRANSACTIONS._description: ATW (At The Window) - Cash transactions at bank branch counter. Both credit and debit are bank transfers with cash group.

NEFT_TRANSACTIONS._description: NEFT - Digital bank transfers. Both credit and debit use digital transfer group.

INSURANCE_TRANSACTIONS._description: Insurance transactions. Direction-aware: credit=insurance claim/payout, debit=insurance premium payment.
